title: W. P. Carey Increases Quarterly Dividend to $0.940 per Share
author: Prnewswire
description: W. P. Carey Inc. (NYSE: WPC) announced that its Board of Directors increased the quarterly cash dividend to $0.940 per share, equivalent to an annualized rate of $3.76 per share, payable on July 15, 2026. The net lease REIT operates a portfolio of 1,703 properties covering approximately 185 million square feet, primarily focused on industrial, warehouse, and retail properties in the U.S. and Europe.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company announced a dividend increase from the previous rate to $0.940 per share, demonstrating financial strength and confidence in future cash flows. Dividend increases are typically viewed positively by investors as they indicate management's confidence in the company's ability to generate sustainable earnings and return capital to shareholders.

title: Realty Income and W.P. Carey: Perfect Together
author: Reuben Gregg Brewer
description: The article argues that owning both Realty Income and W.P. Carey, the two largest net lease REITs, complements rather than duplicates investment exposure. While both use similar net lease business models, their portfolios differ significantly: Realty Income focuses on retail properties (80% of rents) while W.P. Carey emphasizes industrial assets (67% of rents). Both offer attractive dividend yields around 5.2-5.3%, making them suitable for income-focused investors seeking a balanced property portfolio.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Positioned as the largest net lease REIT with a strong focus on single-tenant retail properties. Highlighted for attractive 5.15% dividend yield and complementary portfolio characteristics when paired with W.P. Carey. Author personally owns the stock.

Presented as the second-largest net lease REIT with a portfolio tilted toward industrial assets, offering portfolio diversification benefits. Features a competitive 5.12% dividend yield and complements Realty Income's retail focus. Author personally owns the stock.

title: W. P. Carey Increases Quarterly Dividend to $0.930 per Share
author: Prnewswire
description: W. P. Carey Inc. (NYSE: WPC) announced that its Board of Directors increased the quarterly cash dividend to $0.930 per share, equivalent to an annualized rate of $3.72 per share. The dividend is payable on April 15, 2026 to shareholders of record as of March 31, 2026. The company operates a diversified portfolio of 1,682 net lease properties covering approximately 183 million square feet.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company announced a dividend increase from the previous rate to $0.930 per share, demonstrating financial strength and confidence in future cash flows. Dividend increases are typically viewed positively by investors as they indicate management's confidence in the company's profitability and ability to return capital to shareholders.

title: 2 REITs with ’Mafioso’ Economics Yielding Up to 5.2%
author: Brett Owens
description: The article highlights two net-lease REITs as attractive income investments in a lower interest rate environment. W.P. Carey (WPC) and Agree Realty (ADC) offer yields of 5.2% and 4.2% respectively, with strong fundamentals including high occupancy rates, quality tenants, and growth prospects. As the Fed cuts rates, these dividend-paying REITs are positioned to benefit from investor rotation out of money markets and into dividend payers.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: WPC successfully spun off office properties into NLOP, returning to its core industrial and retail net-lease business. The company achieved record $2.1 billion in investments at 9.2% yield in 2025, maintains 97% occupancy, and benefits from contractual rent escalators. The market is undervaluing the company's recovery.

ADC offers a 4.2% monthly dividend with a high-quality portfolio of recession-proof retailers (Walmart, Home Depot, Tractor Supply). The company is aggressively expanding with 305 property acquisitions in 2025 and growing ground lease strategy. Conservative balance sheet and quality tenants provide stability.

ADC offers a 4.2% monthly dividend with a high-quality portfolio of recession-proof retailers (Walmart, Home Depot, Tractor Supply). The company is aggressively expanding with 305 property acquisitions in 2025 and growing ground lease strategy. Conservative balance sheet and quality tenants provide stability.
Mentioned as a reference point for monthly dividend REITs but not recommended. The article suggests ADC has a sharper portfolio and more conservative balance sheet compared to Realty Income.

title: The First 3 Passive Income Investments I Plan to Make In February
author: Matt Dilallo
description: An investor outlines three passive income investments for February: Energy Transfer (a high-yielding MLP with 7.3% yield and 3-5% annual distribution growth), the Schwab U.S. Dividend Equity ETF (3.8% yield tracking 100 dividend growth stocks), and W.P. Carey (a REIT with 5.2% yield investing in industrial and warehouse properties). All three offer steadily rising income streams supported by strong fundamentals and growth prospects.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: High yield of 7.3%, consistent quarterly distribution increases targeting 3-5% annual growth, stable cash flow (90% from fees), conservative 50% payout ratio, and $5-5.5B investment backlog supporting future increases.

High yield of 7.3%, consistent quarterly distribution increases targeting 3-5% annual growth, stable cash flow (90% from fees), conservative 50% payout ratio, and $5-5.5B investment backlog supporting future increases.

Attractive 3.8% trailing yield, tracks 100 high-quality dividend growth stocks, underlying holdings have increased dividends by 8%+ annually over five years, includes companies with long dividend growth streaks (Chevron 39 years, Coca-Cola 63 years).

5.2% dividend yield, record $2.1B invested in new properties last year, net leases provide predictable rising rental income with built-in escalations, quarterly dividend increases (4.5% last year), strong financial profile for continued portfolio expansion.
